# WebWaka OS — Verticals Execution Prompts: Civic Extended + Politics Remaining

**Document type:** Agent execution prompt set
**Scope:** Civic extended + politics remaining verticals — 10 verticals covered
**Repo:** https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os
**Branch base:** `main`
**Milestone targets:** M8d–M12
**Status:** Ready for agent execution — P1 Civic verticals complete (M8d)
**Generated by:** MASTER_CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md — Set E

---

> **3-in-1 Platform Note:**
> Civic verticals primarily serve **Pillar 1 (Ops)** and **Pillar 3 (Marketplace/Discovery)**.
> Political verticals (campaign-office, constituency-office, ward-rep) also activate **Pillar 2 (Branding)** for campaign and constituency sites.
> **SuperAgent AI is cross-cutting — NOT a fourth pillar.**
> **CRITICAL AI AUTONOMY CAPS for this set:**
> - Political content: **L3 HITL maximum** — no AI output about campaign, candidate, or constituency data may be published without human review
> - Civic/community content: L2 standard cap with NDPR consent
> - USSD excluded from all AI calls (P12)
> See `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md` and `docs/governance/ai-architecture-decision-log.md` (ADL political content rule).

### General rules for all agents using these prompts

- Never make assumptions about WebWaka's architecture. Always read the referenced documents and code first.
- Research deeply — especially IT (Incorporated Trustees) registration for faith and civic bodies, CAC for associations, INEC rules for political verticals.
- Every monetary value must be an integer in kobo (P9). Donation amounts, dues, and member contributions must not use floats.
- No cross-tenant data leakage (T3).
- Political content AI: L3 HITL mandatory — read ADL entry in `docs/governance/ai-architecture-decision-log.md` before implementing any AI route for political verticals.

---

## TASK V-CIV-EXT-1: Mosque / Islamic Centre Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `mosque`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Nigeria has the largest Muslim population in sub-Saharan Africa; over 100,000 mosques across northern states and Southwest; NSCIA (Nigeria Supreme Council for Islamic Affairs) and IT registration
- **Milestone target:** M8d
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md
  - Church vertical (M8d reference): https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals-church/

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in Islamic community management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian mosque and Islamic centre operations — Jumu'ah (Friday prayer) congregations, Ramadan programmes, Zakat management (zakat_amount_kobo stored as integer), Sadaqah collection, Eid prayer grounds management, NSCIA affiliation tracking
- IT (Incorporated Trustees) registration process (CAC Abuja) for religious organisations in Nigeria
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine
- Reference M8d church vertical in `packages/verticals-church/` for parallel structure guidance

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — Civic verticals section; entry for `mosque`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `mosque`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine; `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`; `infra/db/migrations/` — next number
- `packages/verticals-church/src/` — reference M8d church implementation for pattern guidance

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian mosque landscape — NSCIA membership, mosques per state (Kano, Lagos, Oyo, Kaduna highest density), average congregation size, Zakat collection volumes during Ramadan
- Competitor Islamic management software: no dominant Nigeria-specific solution; manual ledgers, WhatsApp groups for programmes
- Key features: congregation register, Jumu'ah attendance tracking, Zakat and Sadaqah collection (amount_kobo, donor anonymous flag, date), Ramadan programme schedule, Eid prayer ground booking, IT registration tracking, NSCIA affiliation record

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give every Nigerian mosque a digital congregation management system — attendance, Zakat/Sadaqah collection, programme scheduling, and IT compliance tracking — all monetary in kobo
- **Schema:**
  - `mosque_profiles` — mosque_name, NSCIA_affiliation_number, IT_registration_number, state, lga, congregation_size, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `mosque_donations` — donor_anonymous BOOLEAN, donor_phone (nullable for anonymous), donation_type (zakat/sadaqah/waqf/general), amount_kobo, donation_date, tenant_id
  - `mosque_programmes` — programme_name, type (jumu'ah/tarawih/eid/lecture/ramadan), scheduled_date INTEGER, attendance_count INTEGER, tenant_id
  - `mosque_members` — member_phone, member_name, zakat_eligible BOOLEAN, joined_date INTEGER, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `it_registered` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `MEMBER_ENGAGEMENT_REPORT` (congregation engagement trend), `DONATION_TREND` (Zakat/Sadaqah seasonal pattern) — L2; Growth plan; NDPR consent required; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** IT registration number at `it_registered`; NSCIA affiliation stored on profile
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for donation recording; Tier 2 for Waqf/endowment fund management above ₦1M

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-mosque` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_mosque.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Donor phone nullable for anonymous donations.

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-mosque/src/index.ts` — `registerMosqueVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate donation totals and programme attendance counts — anonymous donations remain anonymous; no member PII to AI (P13). L2 cap.

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/mosque.ts`. AI blocked on USSD (P12). Mount in `apps/api/src/index.ts`.

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped to `tenant_id`.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-mosque/src/mosque.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `it_registered` with valid IT number; donation with integer amount_kobo (P9); anonymous donation stores no donor_phone; programme scheduled; congregation attendance tracked; AI engagement report with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI triply blocked; `it_registered` without IT number → typed error; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/mosque): Mosque vertical — congregation management, Zakat/Sadaqah, IT registration (M8d)"
git push origin feat/vertical-mosque
```
PR checklist:
- [ ] Vertical registered in FSM
- [ ] Migration created correctly — donor_phone nullable for anonymous donations
- [ ] `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- [ ] PR labeled: `3in1:pillar-1`, `3in1:pillar-2`, `3in1:pillar-3`
- [ ] Anonymous donation anonymity preserved — no donor PII to AI (P13)
- [ ] AI routes use SuperAgent L2 autonomy
- [ ] USSD exclusion enforced (P12)
- [ ] T3 enforced
- [ ] Tests ≥ 15, passing
- [ ] Zero typecheck/lint errors

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `youth-organization`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Nigeria has active youth organizations at federal, state, LGA, and community levels; NYSC, student unions, NUS, NANS, FUTA/UI/UNILAG student associations

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in civic membership and youth organization management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian youth organization landscape — NYSC (National Youth Service Corps), NANS (National Association of Nigerian Students), NUS (Nigerian Union of Students), state youth boards, community development youth wings, university student unions
- CAC association registration, NYSC coordination, state youth commission awareness
- Membership dues collection in kobo, event coordination, meeting minutes, scholarship fund management
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — Civic verticals; entry for `youth-organization`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4

- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`; `infra/db/migrations/` — next number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks:
- Nigerian youth organization ecosystem — NANS, NUS membership, state youth boards, university student union structures, typical annual dues range (₦500–₦10,000)
- Key features: member register, dues collection (annual_dues_kobo, payment_status), meeting minute records, event/programme coordination, scholarship fund log (donor_amount_kobo, recipient, award_amount_kobo), executive election results reference

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian youth organizations a digital member register, dues collection, event coordination, and scholarship fund management — all monetary in kobo
- **Schema:**
  - `youth_org_profiles` — org_name, type (student_union/community_youth/nysc_cda/political_youth_wing), CAC_reg_number, NYSC_coordination, state, lga, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `youth_org_members` — member_phone, member_name, membership_year INTEGER, annual_dues_kobo, dues_paid BOOLEAN, tenant_id
  - `youth_org_events` — event_name, event_date INTEGER, venue, description, attendance_count INTEGER, tenant_id
  - `youth_org_scholarship` — donor_phone, donor_name, donated_amount_kobo, recipient_name, award_amount_kobo, academic_year, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `MEMBER_ENGAGEMENT_REPORT` (dues compliance rate, event participation) — L2; Growth plan; NDPR consent
- **Regulatory hook:** CAC registration number at `cac_verified`
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for dues and donations; Tier 2 for scholarship disbursements above ₦500,000

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-youth-organization`. `registerYouthOrganizationVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). AI: aggregate dues compliance rate and event counts — no member names, phone (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `cac_verified`; member with integer annual_dues_kobo; dues payment status; event created; scholarship with integer donated_amount_kobo, award_amount_kobo; AI engagement with aiRights + NDPR; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/youth-organization): Youth Org vertical — member register, dues, scholarship fund (M8d)"`. PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled, guards, SuperAgent L2, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `MEMBER_ENGAGEMENT_REPORT` (contribution compliance, event attendance) — L2; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for contributions; Tier 2 for welfare loan disbursements above ₦500,000

## TASK V-CIV-EXT-4: Waste Management / Recycler Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `waste-management`
- **Priority:** P2-Top100 — Waste management is a critical urban service in Nigeria; Lagos Waste Management Authority (LAWMA), FMENV, and state environment agencies regulate; recycling economy growing
- **Milestone target:** M11
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in waste management and recycling operations, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian waste management sector — LAWMA (Lagos), AEPB (Abuja), LASEPA, FMENV compliance; waste collection route management, weighbridge tracking (weight in integer kg), recycling material pricing in kobo per kg; estate and community waste contracts.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian waste management market — LAWMA contractor model, FMENV compliance requirements, recycling aggregator growth (WeCyclers, Pakam, ECOBARTER); key features: collection route register, household/estate subscription (monthly_fee_kobo), waste tonnage log (collection_date, weight_kg INTEGER, waste_type), recycling material purchase log (material_type, weight_kg INTEGER, price_per_kg_kobo, supplier), LAWMA/FMENV permit tracking.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `waste_mgmt_profiles` (company_name, LAWMA_or_state_permit, FMENV_cert, CAC_RC, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `waste_collection_routes` (route_name, zone, client_count, truck_id, collection_day, tenant_id); `waste_subscriptions` (client_phone, client_address, route_id, monthly_fee_kobo, payment_status, tenant_id); `waste_tonnage_log` (route_id, collection_date INTEGER, weight_kg INTEGER, waste_type, tenant_id); `recycling_purchases` (material_type (plastic/paper/metal/glass), weight_kg INTEGER, price_per_kg_kobo, supplier_phone, total_kobo, collection_date, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `fmenv_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (waste volume forecast by zone), `SALES_FORECAST` (subscription revenue) — L2; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for subscription billing; Tier 3 for government waste collection contracts above ₦5M

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-waste-management`. `registerWasteManagementVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. Weight in integer kg (no floats). Price in kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). AI: aggregate tonnage and revenue by zone — no client addresses (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `fmenv_verified`; subscription with integer monthly_fee_kobo; tonnage log with integer weight_kg; recycling purchase with integer price_per_kg_kobo, total_kobo; AI forecast with aiRights + NDPR; float weight rejected; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/waste-management): Waste Management vertical — routes, subscriptions, recycling, FMENV compliance (M11)"`. PR checklist standard.

**Skills required:** Nigerian grassroots sports — NFF-affiliated community football clubs, state basketball associations, athletics clubs; NSF registration, player registration, match scheduling, kit and equipment budget in kobo.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: player roster (player_name, position, age_years INTEGER, jersey_number INTEGER), match schedule (opponent, venue, match_date, result), training schedule, club dues (monthly_dues_kobo), kit purchase log (item, cost_kobo), NFF/NSF registration tracking.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `sports_club_profiles` (club_name, sport_type (football/basketball/athletics/other), NSF_affiliation, state_sports_council_reg, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `sports_club_players` (player_name, position, age_years INTEGER, jersey_number INTEGER, monthly_dues_kobo, dues_status, tenant_id); `sports_club_matches` (opponent, venue, match_date INTEGER, result_home INTEGER, result_away INTEGER, status (scheduled/played/cancelled), tenant_id); `sports_club_expenses` (expense_type (kit/equipment/transport/fees), description, amount_kobo, date, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nsf_registered` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `MEMBER_ENGAGEMENT_REPORT` (player attendance, match performance trend) — L2; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for dues; Tier 2 for prize money distribution above ₦200,000

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-sports-club`. `registerSportsClubVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. Kobo (P9). Age and jersey as integers. Tenant scoped (T3). AI: aggregate match results and attendance stats — no player PII (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `nsf_registered`; player with integer age, jersey, monthly_dues_kobo; match with integer result scores; expense with integer amount_kobo; AI trend with aiRights + NDPR; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/sports-club): Sports Club vertical — player roster, match schedule, expenses, AI trend (M12)"`. PR checklist standard.

## TASK V-CIV-EXT-8: Campaign Office Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `campaign-office`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Every Nigerian political candidate runs a campaign office; INEC campaign finance rules require donor disclosure above threshold; L3 HITL mandatory for all AI
- **Milestone target:** M8b
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in political campaign management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian electoral process — INEC (Independent National Electoral Commission) campaign finance rules (Section 91 Electoral Act 2022: ₦1bn presidential, ₦200m governorship campaign spending limits), political party nomination process, ward mobilisation structure, campaign donor disclosure requirements
- **CRITICAL AI GOVERNANCE:** All AI outputs for this vertical must be L3 HITL — no campaign content, voter data analysis, or donor reports may be published without human review; reference `docs/governance/ai-architecture-decision-log.md`
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — Politics verticals; `campaign-office` entry
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `docs/governance/ai-architecture-decision-log.md` — **READ FULLY** — political AI autonomy cap; L3 HITL rule
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `campaign-office`
- `packages/verticals-politician/` — reference M8b politician vertical implementation

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: INEC Electoral Act 2022 campaign finance limits by office level, donor disclosure thresholds, ward mobilisation structure; key features: campaign budget tracker (total_budget_kobo, spent_kobo per category: media/rallies/logistics/materials), donor log (donor_name, donor_phone, amount_kobo — INEC disclosure compliance), volunteer roster, LGA/ward mobilisation tracker, campaign event log (rally, townhall, debate).

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian campaign offices digital budget tracking, donor log (INEC-compliant), volunteer management, and ward mobilisation tracking — all in kobo; all AI outputs L3 HITL
- **Schema:**
  - `campaign_office_profiles` — candidate_name, party, office_sought (president/governor/senator/rep/councillor), INEC_filing_ref, workspace_id, tenant_id, status
  - `campaign_budget` — category (media/rallies/logistics/materials/personnel), budget_kobo, spent_kobo, tenant_id
  - `campaign_donors` — donor_name, donor_phone, amount_kobo, donation_date, inec_disclosure_required BOOLEAN, tenant_id
  - `campaign_volunteers` — volunteer_phone, volunteer_name, lga, ward, role, tenant_id
  - `campaign_events` — event_type (rally/townhall/doorstep), location, lga, event_date INTEGER, estimated_attendance INTEGER, tenant_id
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `inec_filed` → `active` → `campaign_closed`
- **SuperAgent:** `CAMPAIGN_INSIGHT` — **L3 HITL MANDATORY** — no campaign insight, donor analysis, or voter pattern output may be published without explicit human review; NDPR consent required; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** INEC filing reference at `inec_filed`; INEC spending cap check at `active` (block if total_budget_kobo exceeds INEC limit for office_sought)
- **KYC:** Tier 3 — campaign finance involves large value transactions; full identity verification mandatory

---

**3. Implementation:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-campaign-office`. `registerCampaignOfficeVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. All monetary in kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). **L3 HITL flag mandatory on all SuperAgent calls**. Donor phone and name must never be aggregated into AI prompts — only total donation count and anonymised amounts (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-campaign-office/src/campaign-office.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `inec_filed` with filing ref; budget category with integer spent_kobo (P9); donor with integer amount_kobo; volunteer added; event logged; AI campaign insight with HITL flag set + aiRights + NDPR. Negative: INEC spending cap exceeded → typed error; fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI without HITL flag → blocked; AI with aiRights false → blocked; NDPR absent → blocked; USSD → blocked; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; Tier 3 KYC → 403 for lower tiers.

**CRITICAL TEST:** AI call without `autonomy_level: "L3_HITL"` must return an error — this is a hard block.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/campaign-office): Campaign Office vertical — budget tracking, donor log, INEC compliance, L3 HITL AI (M8b)"
git push origin feat/vertical-campaign-office
```
PR checklist:
- [ ] L3 HITL flag set and tested on all AI routes — **blocking if missing**
- [ ] INEC spending cap enforcement tested per office_sought
- [ ] Donor PII excluded from all AI aggregations (P13)
- [ ] `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- [ ] PR labeled: `3in1:pillar-1`, `3in1:pillar-2`, `3in1:pillar-3`
- [ ] Tier 3 KYC enforced
- [ ] T3 on all queries; USSD blocked; NDPR gated
- [ ] Tests ≥ 15; zero typecheck/lint errors

- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in legislative constituency management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian legislative constituency management — NDDC (Niger Delta Development Commission) funds, constituency development funds (CDF), constituency outreach, constituent complaint management, NASS transparency requirements; all project funds in kobo; **L3 HITL for all AI on constituent data or fund analysis**.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents plus `docs/governance/ai-architecture-decision-log.md` for political AI cap.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: constituency profile (legislator name, office (senator/rep), state, constituency), project register (project_name, category (road/school/borehole/health), allocated_kobo, disbursed_kobo, status, LGA), constituent complaint log (complaint_ref, LGA, ward, subject, status — no complainant details to AI), outreach event log (date, attendees_count INTEGER, LGA).

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `constituency_office_profiles` (legislator_name, office_type (senator/rep/state_assembly), constituency_name, inec_seat_number, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `constituency_projects` (project_name, category, lga, allocated_kobo, disbursed_kobo, contractor, status (planned/funded/in_progress/completed), tenant_id); `constituency_complaints` (complaint_ref TEXT, lga, ward, subject, description, status (received/assigned/resolved), assigned_to, tenant_id); `constituency_outreach` (event_date INTEGER, lga, event_type, attendees_count INTEGER, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `inec_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `CAMPAIGN_INSIGHT` (constituent engagement trend) — **L3 HITL MANDATORY**; NDPR consent; USSD excluded (P12)
- **KYC:** Tier 3 — public fund management

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-constituency-office`. `registerConstituencyOfficeVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). L3 HITL on all AI. No complainant PII to AI (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `inec_verified`; project with integer allocated_kobo, disbursed_kobo; complaint logged; outreach with integer attendees_count; AI engagement with HITL flag + aiRights + NDPR; missing HITL flag → blocked; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; Tier 3 → 403 for lower. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/constituency-office): Constituency Office vertical — projects, complaints, outreach, L3 HITL AI (M12)"`. PR checklist: L3 HITL mandatory, P13 complainant exclusion, Tier 3 KYC, standard checks.

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in grassroots political representation management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian ward-level governance — INEC ward delineation (36 states × ~12,000 wards), councillor operations, LGA administration, polling unit register, community meeting records; simplified operations; **L3 HITL for all AI** (political content cap); Tier 1 KYC for basic operations.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `ward_rep_profiles` (councillor_name, ward_name, lga, state, inec_ward_code, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `ward_polling_units` (unit_number TEXT, address, registered_voters INTEGER, tenant_id); `ward_projects` (project_name, category, amount_kobo, status (planned/in_progress/completed), tenant_id); `ward_service_requests` (request_type, description, ward, status (received/resolved), tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `active` (3-state; simplified for grassroots level)
- **SuperAgent:** `CAMPAIGN_INSIGHT` (ward constituent engagement) — **L3 HITL MANDATORY**; NDPR; USSD excluded (P12)
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for basic; Tier 2 for project fund management

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM 3-state; polling unit with integer registered_voters; project with integer amount_kobo; service request logged; AI ward insight with HITL flag + aiRights + NDPR; HITL missing → blocked; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401. **Minimum: 15 tests.**
